caption: Cycle charge against a target that breaks and flees: the first impact routs the infantry, and the cavalry now retires at a trot, brakes onto the 280 wu standoff instead of galloping through the turn-around with full momentum, then canters back in and lands a second charge on the fleeing target around t950-1000. Previously the pursuer receded away from the router indefinitely -- the peel galloped past the standoff, the soldier bodies dragged the regiment further out, and the walk-pace re-approach could never close on a target fleeing at nearly the same speed.
